behavior_summary: This script implements event handlers for an east-west two-tile door object. The handlers do minimal local work and delegate the actual logic to shared DOOR_NS routines by spawning them as processes.

Event handlers covered: U8P_TDOOR_EW::look (examine), use (activate/open), and gotHit (receive damage). Each handler prepares a small piece of door-identifying info, excludes the object from normal processing, then spawns a DOOR_NS handler to perform the real behavior and returns that handler's result.
U8P_TDOOR_EW::look: prepares info (push constant 0x0208 + indirect 2-byte info), runs process-exclude, then spawns DOOR_NS::00D0 with (info, self) to perform inspection-related behavior.

use: prepares info (push constant 0x0207 + indirect 2-byte info), runs process-exclude, pushes a constant byte 0x01 (inferred mode flag), then spawns DOOR_NS::08B4 with (use_flag, info, self) to perform interaction/opening logic.

gotHit: prepares info (push constant 0x020C + indirect 2-byte info), runs process-exclude, forwards the incoming damage word parameter, then spawns DOOR_NS::0C82 with (damage, info, self) to centralize damage response (breakage/sound/state change).
No global flags are read or written in this excerpt; object-context and small immediate parameters are used to communicate state to DOOR_NS handlers.
notable_control_flow: Each event handler runs synchronously until it spawns a DOOR_NS handler; the spawn return value is captured into a temporary and then returned to the caller.

Before delegating, every handler sets an info key (different constant per event) and runs a 'process exclude' operation on the object — implying the object is marked or protected while the spawned handler runs.
Delegation centralizes door behavior into DOOR_NS: this file acts as a thin adapter translating event-specific parameters into the shared handler's calling convention.
open_questions: Precise meanings of the pushed info constants (0x0208, 0x0207, 0x020C) and how DOOR_NS interprets the indirect 2-byte info remain unspecified.
Exact semantics of the constant byte 0x01 passed from use() — inferred to be a mode/option but the possible modes and effects are unknown.
Whether 'process exclude' prevents concurrent interactions or is purely a bookkeeping flag for the spawned DOOR_NS routines is not explicit.
How the returned spawn retval is used by the engine or caller (beyond being returned) is not detailed in the excerpt.

main_function.name: U8P_TDOOR_EW::look
main_function.purpose: Handle 'U8P_TDOOR_EW::look' (examine) events for an east-west two-tile door by preparing door info/state, excluding the object from normal processing, and spawning a shared DOOR_NS handler (DOOR_NS::00D0) to perform the inspection behavior. Returns the spawned handler's result.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_TDOOR_EW::look) — invoked when the avatar or relevant actor looks at or inspects this door object.

function_map.name: U8P_TDOOR_EW::look
function_map.role: Prepare door info and delegate 'U8P_TDOOR_EW::look' behavior to DOOR_NS::00D0 (spawned).

function_map.name: U8P_TDOOR_EW::use
function_map.role: Prepare door info, push a use-mode flag (0x01), and delegate 'use' behavior to DOOR_NS::08B4 (spawned).

function_map.name: U8P_TDOOR_EW::gotHit
function_map.role: Forward an incoming damage/hit parameter plus door info to DOOR_NS::0C82 (spawned) to handle hit/damage reactions.

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::00D0
function_map.role: Shared handler for door 'U8P_TDOOR_EW::look' behavior (called via spawn).

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::08B4
function_map.role: Shared handler for door 'use' behavior (called via spawn).

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::0C82
function_map.role: Shared handler for door 'gotHit' (damage) behavior (called via spawn).

external_calls.purpose: Delegate door-inspection/U8P_TDOOR_EW::look processing using prepared door info and the door object.
Delegate use/interaction logic; forwards a constant use flag (0x01), the door info, and the door object to the shared use handler.
Delegate damage/hit handling; forwards the incoming damage value, door info, and the door object to the shared damage handler.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements event handlers for an east-west two-tile door object. The handlers do minimal local work and delegate the actual logic to shared DOOR_NS routines by spawning them as processes.
